description: Multi-component interaction: Consumer offset commit race during cooperative rebalance causes consumer to be kicked out of group

required_findings.description: Identifies ConsumerCoordinator as the component that manages rebalancing and coordinates offset commits during onJoinPrepare
Identifies onJoinPrepare as the callback invoked before joining the consumer group, which triggers offset commits for auto-commit
Identifies the poll() loop as the component that calls ConsumerCoordinator and triggers rebalancing
Explains that onJoinPrepare was changed from synchronous to asynchronous offset commit (after KAFKA-13310) to avoid blocking poll() for too long
Identifies that the async commit completion is never properly tracked, causing onJoinPrepare to be called repeatedly on each poll()
Explains that each poll() invokes onJoinPrepare again and initiates a NEW async offset commit because the completion flag remains false
Identifies that the consumer is kicked out of the group after hitting max.poll.interval.ms or rebalance.timeout.ms because poll() takes too long
Explains that the issue only affects cooperative rebalancing (CooperativeStickyAssignor), not eager rebalancing
Identifies that the fix involves promoting the commit future to an instance variable (rebalanceAutoCommitFuture) that persists across poll() calls
Mentions that network latency or slow offset commits exacerbate the issue by making it more likely that commits don't finish before the next poll()
Identifies auto-commit (enable.auto.commit=true) as a precondition for the bug
Explains that the local future variable in onJoinPrepare is lost between poll() calls, so the completion handler cannot update any shared state

file_references.description: Identifies clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/internals/AbstractCoordinator.java (where group coordination and rebalancing logic live)
Identifies test files like clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/internals/ConsumerCoordinatorTest.java or integration tests
References the related KAFKA-13310 PR or commit that introduced the async commit change
References KAFKA-14024 or PR #12349 as the fix for this issue

causal_chain.description: Component 1 (ConsumerCoordinator): Rebalance is triggered (consumer joins/leaves group, or partitions change). ConsumerCoordinator invokes onJoinPrepare callback before sending JoinGroup request. Since auto-commit is enabled, onJoinPrepare initiates an async offset commit for current offsets.

Timing gap: The async offset commit is slow (network latency, broker load) and does NOT complete before the next poll() call. The local future variable in onJoinPrepare is lost when the method returns, so there is NO way for the completion handler to update shared state.

Component 2 (Poll loop): Consumer calls poll() again (because poll() is non-blocking and applications typically call it in a loop). Since the completion flag was never updated, ConsumerCoordinator sees that rebalancing is still pending and invokes onJoinPrepare AGAIN.

Component 3 (Rebalance timeout): Each poll() initiates a NEW async offset commit, creating a loop where commits are repeatedly sent but never complete in time. poll(Duration) exceeds its timeout and the consumer is kicked out of the group after max.poll.interval.ms or rebalance.timeout.ms.

negative_checks.description: Does NOT blame the Kafka broker or offset commit protocol as the root cause
Does NOT claim the bug is in the eager rebalancing assignor (RangeAssignor, RoundRobinAssignor, StickyAssignor)
